name: corrected-wide-v3-external-drat-v1

selection.algorithm: select the lexicographically lowest content hash among all expected-unsat rows

exporter.source_policy: build and run from a clean detached commit containing this registration

external_checker.makefile_note: the pinned upstream Makefile command omits the feature macro needed to declare getc_unlocked under GCC 15.2.0; the source is unchanged
external_checker.positive_gate: exit zero and stdout contains s VERIFIED
external_checker.negative_control: remove only the final textual DRAT line, rerun the same checker, and require that stdout does not contain s VERIFIED

preservation.access_control: do not commit the source query, DIMACS, DRAT, or LRAT bytes
retain: source, exporter, checker, DIMACS, DRAT, optional LRAT, and manifest SHA-256 values
exact positive and negative checker stdout/stderr and exit codes

claim_limits: This cell tests standard external consumption of one preregistered real clausal proof; it is not a proof-coverage estimate.
DRAT verification establishes CNF unsatisfiability, not source-to-CNF faithfulness by itself.
Checker timing is diagnostic and not solver-performance evidence.
The result cannot change concretization policy, finding recall, or the symbolic-memory gate.
